goal: Make [check:X] column actually useful: cascade phase2 judgment > phase1 warning > default; cache phase1 warnings to .ml-cache/views/phase1-warnings.json; omit the column for already-merged intents.

summary.title: [check:X] log column: cascade phase2 > phase1 > default; drop the column for terminal-state intents
summary.what: Replaces the dead `[check:-]` column in `mainline log` with a small priority cascade. checkMarker now takes (status, LastCheck, hasPhase1Warning) and returns '' (drop the segment entirely) for merged/abandoned/superseded/reverted intents; '!' / 'human?' / 'ok' for the three phase2 outcomes; '~' for intents the latest sync auto-check still flags via phase1 fingerprint overlap but no agent has judged yet; '?' as the default for proposed/sealed_local intents with no signal at all. Sync persists the full active pair set from detectSyncConflicts to .ml-cache/views/phase1-warnings.json so the log render answers 'does this intent currently have a phase1 warning?' with one cache lookup. The CLI log row drops the entire ' [check:X]' segment when the marker is empty — a merged intent's row is now visibly shorter and the eye scans straight to titles. Storage's ReadTeamConfig backfills the rc5+ Sync defaults (FreshnessSeconds, StaleThresholdSeconds, AutoCheckAfterSync) for repos initialised pre-rc5 so the auto-check branch fires on existing checkouts without a manual config edit.
summary.why: Dogfood feedback: 13 of 13 visible intents in this repo showed `[check:-]` because phase2 (mainline check --submit) is almost never triggered manually — there is no enforcement node that drives reviewers to it. The column was honest about a gap but useless to look at. Two design moves shut the gap: (a) repurpose the column as 'should reviewer pay attention?' instead of 'has phase2 been run?', so phase1 sync warnings (already produced by rc5 auto-check, currently thrown away) get a light surface; (b) drop the column entirely on terminal-state intents because they cannot be 'paid attention to'. The phase1 vs phase2 semantic distinction is preserved by giving each its own marker (`~` vs `ok`/`!`/`human?`) and by keeping phase2 strictly higher priority — agents that take the time to run phase2 are not overruled by a phase1 hint.
summary.user_goal: Make mainline log scannable for reviewers — at a glance, which proposed intents need attention?

decisions.point: Phase1 vs phase2 semantics in one column
decisions.chose: Distinct markers ~ vs ok/!/human?, priority phase2 > phase1
decisions.rationale: Phase1 is fingerprint heuristic (transient, may flicker between syncs); phase2 is agent judgment (durable event in actor log). Same column with different glyphs preserves the distinction without forcing the reader to know which subsystem produced the signal.

decisions.point: Dropping the column for merged/terminal states
decisions.chose: checkMarker returns '' and the CLI omits the ' [check:X]' segment
decisions.rationale: Reviewer attention budget is for pre-merge intents. The status column already says 'merged'; a check marker on a terminal intent reads as residue. Empty marker shortens the row visibly.

decisions.chose: Full pair list, single file at .ml-cache/views/phase1-warnings.json, overwritten on every sync
decisions.rationale: Phase1 results are transient — next sync may add or drop pairs as actor logs evolve. Append-only would accumulate stale warnings forever. Overwrite-on-sync is the simplest expression of 'snapshot of right now'.
rejected: Persist phase1 results into the actor log as events — would conflate transient heuristic output with durable sealed/abandoned/check-judgment events; bloats the log.
Per-intent files — increases inode pressure for no scan-time win since the file is small and read once per log render.

decisions.rationale: Sync is the single canonical place where the view is rebuilt; piggybacking the cache write keeps semantics predictable. Seal-time conflict detection still runs (and surfaces conflicts in the seal response) but does not touch the cache — next sync will recompute.

decisions.chose: Apply defaults when fields are zero-valued; explicitlyHasKey scan distinguishes 'missing from file' from 'set to false'
decisions.rationale: Without the backfill, every repo initialised before rc5 silently disabled AutoCheckAfterSync — the phase1 cache would never get written for the dogfood that motivated this PR. Strict 'zero means default' would clobber a deliberate `auto_check_after_sync = false`; the lightweight key-presence scan is enough to tell them apart for rc5+ booleans.

risks: explicitlyHasKey is a regex-free string scan — it will be confused by oddly-formatted TOML (e.g. multi-line comments containing 'auto_check_after_sync'). Acceptable risk for a config file mainline writes itself; long-term replace with a real TOML AST walk if a real config gets that complex.

The phase1 cache only refreshes when the user runs sync (or a command that auto-syncs). Between syncs, a `~` marker may persist for an intent whose remote half has since been merged. Rebuilds happen often enough in normal use that this is small; no caller treats `~` as durable truth.
If two reviewers run `mainline log` on different machines they may see different markers because each machine has its own phase1 cache. Acceptable: phase1 is local heuristic, not global truth.
followups: After a few weeks of dogfood, look at how often `~` actually drives a reviewer to run `mainline check`. If they ignore it, either the threshold is too noisy (tighten phase1) or the marker is in the wrong column (rethink).
Promote this design to v0.1-rc6 patch text once the dogfood data is in.
Consider showing the `~` marker on `mainline status` too, so an intent author sees that the team's view of their work has surfaced an overlap.

architectural_claims: checkMarker is the single source of truth for the [check:X] column; CLI render does no logic beyond format.
Phase1 conflict cache lifetime is one sync cycle: every Sync writes the full snapshot; nothing else writes to it.
Terminal-state intents do not participate in the [check:X] column — the status field already conveys 'no further attention required'.
behavioral_changes: mainline log shows [check:?] / [check:~] / [check:ok] / [check:!] / [check:human?] for pre-merge intents, and no [check:...] segment at all for merged/abandoned/superseded/reverted ones.
mainline sync writes .ml-cache/views/phase1-warnings.json as part of the auto-check branch.
Repos initialised before rc5 now get AutoCheckAfterSync / FreshnessSeconds / StaleThresholdSeconds defaults at read-time without needing a config edit.

api_changes.signature: engine.checkMarker(status domain.IntentStatus, lc *domain.CheckSummary, hasPhase1Warning bool) string

data_model_changes.name: domain.Phase1WarningsCache at .ml-cache/views/phase1-warnings.json
data_model_changes.migration_notes: Created on first sync after upgrade; absence is treated as 'no warnings cached'.

migration_notes: Pre-rc5 .mainline/config.toml files keep working; ReadTeamConfig now backfills missing rc5+ defaults rather than silently falling to zero values.
